content.problemStatement:
Chef Ceil has some matchsticks in his kitchen.

**Detail of matchsticks:**

There are NNN matchsticks in total. They are numbered from to 000 to N−1N-1N−1 inclusive. The ithi^{th}ith matchstick takes bib_{i}bi​ time to burn when lighted at one end, and it burns at a uniform rate.

If lighted at both ends simultaneously, the matchstick will take only half of the original time to burn down.

**Arrangement:**

He ties rear end of the all the matchsticks together at one point and the front end is kept free. The matchstick numbered iii is adjacent to matchstick numbered i+1i+1i+1 for all 0≤i≤N−20 \leq i \leq N-20≤i≤N−2.

Bodies of matchsticks do not touch each other, except at the rear end.

**Task:**

There are QQQ queries, in each query we ask:
If he lights the free end of all matchsticks numbered between LLL and RRR inclusive, what will be the time needed for all matchsticks to get completely burnt?
content.inputFormat:
First line of input contains a single integer NNN.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers, the ithi^{th}ith of which is bib_ibi​
The next line contains a single integer QQQ
The next QQQ lines each contain two space separated integers - LLL and RRR. The ithi^{th}ith line represents the ithi^{th}ith query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print the answer on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 3: , in the figure above, yellow colored matches are lighted by a lighter simultaneously.
The numbers indicate the time required to burn that matchstick (if lighted at one end).
Now the first lighted matchstick will completely burn in 555 seconds. Then it will light up all the rest matches from the rear end.
Some matches will have fire at both ends and thus after 555 seconds, they will start burning with twice the original rate.
Thus time taken for matches to burn completely will be :
(from left to right):  8.08.08.0, 9.09.09.0, 7.07.07.0, 6.06.06.0, 5.05.05.0, 6.06.06.0, 7.07.07.0, 6.06.06.0, 7.57.57.5, 5.05.05.0, 8.58.58.5, 8.08.08.0, 6.06.06.0, 6.06.06.0, 7.07.07.0, 6.06.06.0, 8.08.08.0, 7.07.07.0.
So, the answer will be 9.09.09.0 (the maximum among these).
